summary: Frontend-only validation completed for Surah reader layout, listening tab playback controls, and recitation test mic flow on Chromium desktop/mobile emulation.

ui_bugs.issue: React runtime warning appears repeatedly: 'Cannot update a component while rendering a different component' involving SurahReaderPage/AchievementUnlockNotification.
Critical interactive controls (play/pause, speed/repeat/timer/reciter buttons) are missing data-testid attributes, reducing test reliability and regression coverage.

integration_issues.issue: Not fully verifiable in this environment (Chromium only). Playback works in emulation, but physical iOS Safari/Android Chrome validation is still required for user-reported device failure.
Could not reproduce clear transcript duplication in this run; requires real microphone speech stream/device testing to confirm final behavior under Web Speech variability.

action_items: Fix setState-during-render warning path triggered from SurahReaderPage/AchievementUnlockNotification.
Add data-testid attributes to ListeningTab interactive controls (play/pause, reciter, speed, repeat, timer, ayah list rows).
Run manual real-device validation on iOS Safari + Android Chrome for /surah/7?mode=listening with play/pause and resume cycles.
Run manual microphone-based verification on /hifz/test to confirm no transcript duplication across first and subsequent utterances.
critical_code_review_comments: SurahReaderPage is very large (~780+ lines) and should be split to reduce render side-effects and improve maintainability.
ListeningTab lacks data-testid coverage for key actions, violating testability guideline for critical interactions.
Recitation footer text says 2s silence while SILENCE_TIMEOUT_MS is 3200ms; align UX copy with actual logic.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Layout shift was not reproducible in desktop (1920x1080) nor mobile emulation (390x844); no horizontal overflow detected in /surah/7 including mushaf mode. Listening playback button switched to pause in mobile emulation, indicating play started. Recitation start/mic/skip/cancel controls worked in browser automation. Remaining risk is device-specific behavior for iOS/Android audio and Web Speech transcript quality.
rca of the issue: Most visible defect from this run is runtime React warning caused by state update during render lifecycle (SurahReaderPage stack trace references AchievementUnlockNotification). For the originally reported mobile listening failure and transcript repetition, code changes appear directionally correct (audio.load() before play retry, overlap merge logic in speech hook), but true RCA confirmation needs physical mobile browser testing because autoplay/mic behavior differs from Chromium emulation.
